libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: When exercising com.mchange.v2.naming.ReferenceableUtils.referenceToObject(...) with overloads that do not pass an explicit allowedFactoryClassNames Set, set the system property com.mchange.v2.naming.objectFactoryWhitelist to the fully qualified test ObjectFactory class name(s) used by the test, for example in a JUnit setup method, and restore/clear it afterward. Prefer a concrete whitelist over '*'. Alternatively, call the overload that accepts an explicit Set containing the test ObjectFactory class name if that still covers the intended path.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #2097 [Automation] java run fails for com.mchange:mchange-commons-java:0.4.0; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: If the property is not restored after the test, it can affect other tests because ReferenceableUtils reads System properties globally.
Using '*' would make the test less representative of the library's intended 0.4.0 security posture.
The artifact has optional logging/config dependencies, but they are not required for the failing JNDI/ObjectFactory coverage path and no Docker service is indicated.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: mchange-commons-java 0.4.0 adds mandatory security configuration for JNDI Reference/ObjectFactory resolution; tests that exercise ReferenceableUtils.referenceToObject overloads without an explicit allowed-factory set must provide com.mchange.v2.naming.objectFactoryWhitelist.
